an4.94:1.1: "These four types of persons, *bhikkhus*, exist and are found in the world.
an4.94:1.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a certain person possesses inner settling of the mind [*samatha*], but does not possess insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*].
an4.94:1.4: Here again, *bhikkhus*, a certain person possesses insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*], but does not possess inner settling of the mind [*samatha*].
an4.94:1.5: Here again, *bhikkhus*, a certain person possesses neither inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] nor insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*].
an4.94:1.6: Here again, *bhikkhus*, a certain person possesses both inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*].
an4.94:2.1: There, *bhikkhus*, the person who possesses inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] but does not possess insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*] — that person should approach the one who possesses insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*], and having approached, should say to them:
an4.94:2.4: How should *saṅkhāras* be seen with insight?'
an4.94:2.5: That one explains according to what they have seen and understood:
an4.94:2.6: 'Thus, friend, should *saṅkhāras* be seen; thus should *saṅkhāras* be investigated; thus should *saṅkhāras* be seen with insight.'
an4.94:2.7: In time, that person comes to possess both inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*].
an4.94:3.1: There, *bhikkhus*, the person who possesses insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*] but does not possess inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] — that person should approach the one who possesses inner settling of the mind [*samatha*], and having approached, should say to them:
an4.94:3.2: 'How, friend, should the mind [*citta*] be steadied?
an4.94:3.3: How should the mind [*citta*] be composed?
an4.94:3.4: How should the mind [*citta*] be made unified [*ekodi*]?
an4.94:3.5: How should the mind [*citta*] be placed in *samādhi*?'
an4.94:3.6: That one explains according to what they have seen and understood:
an4.94:3.7: 'Thus, friend, should the mind [*citta*] be steadied; thus should the mind [*citta*] be composed; thus should the mind [*citta*] be made unified [*ekodi*]; thus should the mind [*citta*] be placed in *samādhi*.'
an4.94:3.8: In time, that person comes to possess both insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*] and inner settling of the mind [*samatha*].
an4.94:4.1: There, *bhikkhus*, the person who possesses neither inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] nor insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*] — that person should approach the one who possesses both inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*], and having approached, should say to them:
an4.94:4.10: 'Thus, friend, should the mind [*citta*] be steadied; thus should the mind [*citta*] be composed; thus should the mind [*citta*] be made unified [*ekodi*]; thus should the mind [*citta*] be placed in *samādhi*; thus should *saṅkhāras* be seen; thus should *saṅkhāras* be investigated; thus should *saṅkhāras* be seen with insight.'
an4.94:4.11: In time, that person comes to possess both inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*].
an4.94:4.2: 'How, friend, should the mind [*citta*] be steadied?
an4.94:4.3: How should the mind [*citta*] be composed?
an4.94:4.4: How should the mind [*citta*] be made unified [*ekodi*]?
an4.94:4.5: How should the mind [*citta*] be placed in *samādhi*?
an4.94:4.8: How should *saṅkhāras* be seen with insight?'
an4.94:4.9: That one explains according to what they have seen and understood:
an4.94:5.1: There, *bhikkhus*, the person who possesses both inner settling of the mind [*samatha*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*paññā*] — that person, standing firm in those very wholesome *dhammas*, should apply further effort for the destruction of the *āsavas*.
an4.94:5.2: These, *bhikkhus*, are the four types of persons that exist and are found in the world."
